title: Implement expand-migrate-contract DB migration strategy
summary: Implements the expand-migrate-contract database migration strategy for Keystone upgrades. Adds UpgradePhase type with 4 phases (Expanding, Migrating, RollingUpdate, Contracting), status fields (installedRelease, targetRelease, upgradePhase), version parsing with sequential upgrade validation, and phased Job execution coordinated with Deployment rollout. Fresh deployments continue using simple db_sync. Comprehensive unit tests cover all phase transitions, edge cases, and error paths. Integration tests verify both fresh deployment tracking and full upgrade cycle. The implementation is clean, well-structured, and follows existing codebase patterns. One lint issue needs fixing: test code accesses the deprecated result.Requeue field directly instead of using the existing test pattern.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./operators/keystone/... clean)
Edge cases covered (interrupted upgrade resume, tag change during upgrade, unparseable versions, patch-only changes)
Version parsing covers valid formats, invalid formats, boundary values (12 test cases)
Sequential upgrade validation covers valid/invalid paths including downgrades, skip-levels (9 test cases)
All 4 phases tested for Job creation, running/requeue, completion/transition, failure
Contract phase explicitly verified to use NEW image tag
Integration test verifies fresh deployment InstalledRelease tracking with no upgrade Jobs
Integration test verifies full expand-migrate-contract cycle with correct image tags per phase
Deployment tests verify RollingUpdate→Contracting transition and regression guards

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing sub-reconciler condition contract with ObservedGeneration
Uses shared buildUpgradeJob to avoid duplication across 3 phase builders
Functions are focused and small (each phase handler ~30 lines)
Job names incorporate CR name for multi-instance safety
Uses existing job.RunJob for idempotent Job execution
golangci-lint clean (no new issues in production code)

security_checklist.item: PSS Restricted security context on all expand/migrate/contract Job containers

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — upgrade logic added to existing reconcileDatabase, coordinated via reconcileDeployment
Phase state machine persisted in status for crash resilience
RollingUpdate pass-through leverages existing sequential sub-reconciler chain pattern
CRD manifests regenerated and include new status fields, printcolumn, and enum validation
No unnecessary abstractions — buildUpgradeJob shared builder is justified by 3 callers

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code (shared buildUpgradeJob, shared test helpers upgradingKeystone/completedUpgradeJob)
No unused code or dead paths
No future-proofing beyond requirements (e.g., no SLURP multi-release upgrade support)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Upgrade path validated before any mutation (parse errors and non-sequential caught in initiateUpgrade)
Tag change during active upgrade detected and blocked before phase dispatch
Unknown upgrade phase returns error immediately

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (version strings parsed with clear error messages)
Unparseable versions in isUpgrade delegate to initiateUpgrade for proper condition setting
Phase transitions are idempotent (RunJob handles existing/completed Jobs)

issues_found.description: Test code accesses deprecated result.Requeue field directly, triggering SA1019 staticcheck warnings. The existing test pattern uses Equal(ctrl.Result{Requeue: true}) which avoids the deprecation warning. Three instances in reconcile_database_test.go and four instances in reconcile_deployment_test.go access the field directly.
issues_found.fix: Replace g.Expect(result.Requeue).To(BeTrue()) with g.Expect(result).To(Equal(ctrl.Result{Requeue: true})) to match existing pattern in reconcile_fernet_test.go:95 and reconcile_credential_test.go:99. Replace g.Expect(result.Requeue).To(BeFalse()) with g.Expect(result.Requeue).To(BeFalse()) — or better, assert the full result value. Affected lines: reconcile_database_test.go:569,897,964,1009 and reconcile_deployment_test.go:989,1051,1086.
issues_found.evidence:
golangci-lint output:
operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database_test.go:569:11: SA1019: result.Requeue is deprecated: Use `RequeueAfter` instead. (staticcheck)
operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database_test.go:897:11: SA1019: result.Requeue is deprecated
operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database_test.go:964:11: SA1019: result.Requeue is deprecated

Existing pattern that avoids the warning (reconcile_fernet_test.go:95):
g.Expect(result).To(Equal(ctrl.Result{Requeue: true}))

issues_found.description: TestKeystoneStatusUpgradeFields (lines 155-166) duplicates the InstalledRelease, TargetRelease, and UpgradePhase zero-value checks already present in the modified TestKeystoneStatusFields (lines 127-135). Both verify the same conditions on a zero-value KeystoneStatus.
issues_found.fix: Remove TestKeystoneStatusUpgradeFields entirely — its assertions are already covered by TestKeystoneStatusFields.

suggested_improvements: Consider documenting the limitation that resource requests/limits are not set on upgrade Jobs (BestEffort QoS) — tracked as CC-0042 per the reference docs but worth a code comment in buildUpgradeJob.
next_steps: Fix SA1019 lint warnings in test files by using Equal(ctrl.Result{Requeue: true}) pattern instead of direct field access
